description: Severity scoring rules from splotch feature extraction

size.description: Splotch diameter in cm (estimated from camera distance + reference calibration)

intensity.description: Color saturation relative to expected hex value (0.0-1.0)

edgeGradient.description: Sharpness of splotch boundary (sharp = well-defined tumor, diffuse = invasive)

satelliteCount.description: Number of distinct satellite splotches near the primary

fadeRate.description: Splotch intensity decay rate per hour (requires two timepoints)

slow.interpretation: Hypoxic core, poor vascularity — may indicate necrotic center

multipleTypes.description: More than one cancer type hex detected in same scan session

severityLevels.action: Schedule routine oncology consultation within 2 weeks
severityLevels.description: Small, well-defined, single-type detection. Likely early-stage.

severityLevels.action: Schedule oncology consultation within 1 week. Bring encrypted scan export.
severityLevels.description: Moderate signal suggesting established tumor. Imaging confirmation recommended.

severityLevels.action: Urgent oncology referral within 48 hours. Multiple concerning features.
severityLevels.description: Large, intense, or multi-satellite detection. Advanced stage likely.

severityLevels.action: EMERGENCY — Seek immediate medical attention. Go to ER or call oncology emergency line.
severityLevels.description: Extreme signal: massive splotch, many satellites, multiple cancer types, or rapid progression between scans.

disclaimers: This scoring system is a triage aid, not a diagnosis.
All detections require clinical confirmation via imaging and biopsy.
False positives may occur from inflammation, infection, or wound healing.
Severity scores are conservative — when in doubt, escalate.
